xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Pathway-phenotype association via mutual information

Source: R/run_info_assoc.R
run_info_assoc.Rd

Rather than testing whether a gene list overlaps a pathway (as in run_info_enrichment()), this function asks: how much does a pathway's aggregate activity, measured across samples, explain a phenotypic outcome?

run_info_assoc(
  expr,
  phenotype,
  gene_sets,
  universe = NULL,
  score = c("mean_z", "pc1"),
  nbins = NULL,
  n_perm = 1000L,
  min_size = 10L,
  max_size = Inf,
  nthreads = 1L,
  seed = NULL
)

Arguments

expr

Numeric matrix of expression values, samples x genes. Column names must be gene symbols.

phenotype

Vector (numeric, factor, or character) of per-sample phenotype labels or values. Length must equal nrow(expr).

gene_sets

Named list of character vectors defining gene set membership (e.g. GO terms, pathways).

universe

Character vector of background genes. Defaults to colnames(expr).

score

Character string: "mean_z" (default) or "pc1". See the Scoring methods section.

nbins

NULL (default, auto-select) or a positive integer number of equal-frequency bins for score discretisation. See the Binning section.

n_perm

Positive integer. Phenotype-label permutations for the null distribution. Default 1000.

min_size

Integer. Minimum gene set size after universe intersection. Default 10.

max_size

Integer. Maximum gene set size after universe intersection. Default Inf. MI naturally down-weights large sets (high pathway coverage compresses marginal entropy), so this is a computational convenience rather than a statistical necessity.

nthreads

Positive integer. Number of threads for the permutation loop via parallel::mclapply. Default 1L (no parallelism). Values > 1 are silently reduced to 1 on Windows where forking is unavailable. On Linux/macOS the expression matrix is shared copy-on-write between workers, so memory overhead per additional thread is negligible.

seed

Optional integer passed to set.seed(). NULL (default) leaves the RNG state untouched.

Value

A data frame with one row per gene set (sorted by padj), containing:

set

Gene set name.

set_size

Members retained after universe intersection.

nbins_used

Actual bin count used for this run.

MI_bits

Observed mutual information in bits.

z_score

MI standardised against the permutation null. NA when null SD is zero.

p_value

Empirical p-value with continuity correction \((1 + B_{\geq obs}) / (n\_perm + 1)\).

padj

BH-adjusted p_value.

null_mean, null_sd

Mean and SD of the permutation null MI distribution.

Details

Each pathway is summarised into a single per-sample activity score (either the mean z-score across member genes, or the first principal component). Mutual information (MI) between that score and the phenotype vector is computed and compared against a label-permutation null, yielding an empirical p-value that is free of distributional assumptions about either the score or the phenotype.

Scoring methods

"mean_z"

Mean of z-scored expression across pathway members. Fast and robust; the default.

"pc1"

First principal component of z-scored expression. Captures co-expression structure within the pathway but is slower. PC1 sign is arbitrary, which does not affect MI (MI is sign-invariant) but would affect any signed downstream use.

Binning and MI bias

MI is estimated from equal-frequency binned pathway scores. All fixed-bin estimators carry a positive bias that shrinks with sample size. Because both observed and null MI values are computed under identical binning, this bias cancels in relative rankings and empirical p-values — but absolute MI_bits values should not be compared across datasets of different sizes.

When nbins = NULL (default), bins are chosen automatically via a composite rule: Rice rule (\(2n^{1/3}\)) capped to ensure an expected frequency of at least 5 per joint cell (not per marginal bin). For continuous phenotype the joint table has \(nbins^2\) cells, giving \(nbins \leq \lfloor\sqrt{n/5}\rfloor\). For binary phenotype the joint table has only \(2 \times nbins\) cells, allowing larger \(nbins\). A hard cap of 20 applies in either case. Pass an explicit integer to override.

No external dependencies

Discretisation and MI estimation are handled by internal helpers, so infotheo is not required.

References

Paninski, L. (2003). Estimation of entropy and mutual information. Neural Computation, 15(6), 1191-1253.

See also

run_info_enrichment() for the gene-list (ORA) flavour, run_enrichment() for classical ORA/GSEA, compute_pathway_activity() to extract per-sample activity scores from selected pathways for downstream analysis.

Examples

set.seed(1)
n_samples <- 60
genes     <- paste0("G", 1:200)
expr      <- matrix(
  rnorm(n_samples * length(genes)),
  nrow     = n_samples,
  dimnames = list(NULL, genes)
)
phenotype <- sample(c("case", "ctrl"), n_samples, replace = TRUE)
gene_sets <- list(
  pathway_A = genes[1:30],
  pathway_B = genes[50:120],
  pathway_C = genes[150:200]
)

res <- run_info_assoc(
  expr      = expr,
  phenotype = phenotype,
  gene_sets = gene_sets,
  n_perm    = 200,
  seed      = 42
)
#> Warning: Some phenotype groups have fewer than 30 samples: ctrl (n=25). Small group sizes reduce MI estimation stability and may inflate false positive rates.
#> Auto-selected nbins = 6 (Rice rule, capped for joint-table density and 20).
head(res)
#>         set set_size nbins_used   MI_bits    z_score   p_value      padj
#> 1 pathway_A       30          6 0.0269718  0.2407529 0.2636816 0.7910448
#> 2 pathway_B       71          6 0.0000000 -0.5255082 1.0000000 1.0000000
#> 3 pathway_C       51          6 0.0000000 -0.5663308 1.0000000 1.0000000
#>    null_mean    null_sd
#> 1 0.01936997 0.03157524
#> 2 0.01318578 0.02509148
#> 3 0.01606853 0.02837305
